content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

SUPERSEDED
by 18702.1(a)(4)

September 8, 1994

Jean E. Yokley
P.O. Box 718
Bonita, CA 91908

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-94-266

Dear Ms. Yokley:

You have requested advice regarding the conflict-of-interest
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1

QUESTION

Your husband is a captain of the Bonita-Sunnyside Fire
Department. If you are elected to the Board of Directors of the
Bonita-Sunnyside Fire Protection District, may you participate in
decisions regarding budget, salary and pension?

CONCLUSION

Based upon the facts provided, you may participate in
district decisions regarding budget, salary and pension.

FACTS

You are a candidate for the Board of Directors of the Bonita-
Sunnyside Fire Protection District ("district") in San Diego
County. Your husband is a captain of the Bonita-Sunnyside Fire
Department. You seek advice as to whether you may participate in
decisions regarding budget, salary and pension for the district.

ANALYSIS

The Act prohibits a public official from making,
participating in making, or otherwise using his or her official
position to influence a governmental decision in which the

1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations, Sections
18000-18954. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

File No. A-94-266
Page 2


official has a financial interest.  Section 87103 provides in
pertinent part:

          An official has a financial interest in a
     decision within the meaning of Section 87100 if it
     is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will
     have a material financial effect, distinguishable
     from its effect on the public generally, on the
     official or a member of his or her immediate family
     or on:

                    *    *    *

          (c)  Any source of income, other than gifts
     and other than loans by a commercial lending
     institution in the regular course of business on
     terms available to the public without regard to
     official status, aggregating two hundred fifty
     dollars ($250) or more in value provided to,
     received by or promised to the public official
     within 12 months prior to the time when the
     decision is made.

                              (Section 87103(c).)

     Your husband is a captain and salaried employee of the
Bonita-Sunnyside Fire Department.  Section 82030(a), which defines
"income," provides that the income of an individual includes any
community property interest in the income of a spouse.  However,
salary and reimbursement for expenses or per diem received from a
state, local or federal government agency is expressly exempted
from the definition of "income" for purposes of the Act.  (Section
82030(b)(2).)  Consequently, the salary your husband receives from
the Bonita-Sunnyside Fire Department is not considered income
under the Act and will not create a conflict of interest for you,
if you are elected to the district board of directors.

     The Act also provides that a conflict of interest may exist
where a decision will result in a public official's personal
expenses, income, assets, or liabilities or those of his or her
immediate family increasing or decreasing by $250.  (Regulation
18702.1(a)(4), copy enclosed.)  However, an official does not have
to disqualify from participating in a governmental decision if the
decision only affects the salary, per diem or reimbursement for
expenses the official or his or her spouse receives from a state
or local government agency.  (Regulation 18702.1(c)(1), copy
enclosed.)  Thus, if a decision comes before the district that
will affect the salary of your husband, you would not be required

File No. A-94-266
Page 3


to disqualify from the decision.²

In summary, your husband's position with the fire department
will not result in a conflict of interest for you, unless a
district decision will result in the hiring, firing, promotion,
demotion or disciplining of your spouse, or the establishment of a
salary for your spouse which is different from salaries paid to
other employees of the fire department in the same job
classification or position.

I trust this answers your question.

Sincerely,

Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel

By: J[illegible] stecher
Counsel, Legal Division

SGC:JS:ak

Enclosures




___________________________
² However, please note that the exception in Regulation
18702.1(c)(i) does not apply to decisions to hire, fire, promote,
demote or discipline an official's spouse, or to set a salary for
an official's spouse which is different from salaries paid to
other employees of the spouse's agency in the same job
classification or position. (Regulation 18702.1(c)(i).)

If you are elected to the Board of Directors of the
